title: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Copilot â€” Which AI Tool Fits Your Business?
description: An honest comparison of the three major AI assistants for Australian businesses. No hype, just practical advice.
content:
## The three contenders

To see AI tools in action before committing, try our [free AI tools](/tools) — they use Claude under the hood.

If you're considering AI tools for your business, you've probably heard of ChatGPT, Claude, and Microsoft Copilot. All three are capable. None of them is universally "the best." The right choice depends entirely on what you need.

Here's an honest breakdown from someone who deploys all three.

## [ChatGPT](https://chat.openai.com) (OpenAI)

**Best for:** General-purpose tasks, creative content, brainstorming, customer-facing applications.

**Strengths:**
- Largest ecosystem of plugins and integrations
- Strong at creative writing and marketing copy
- GPT-4 handles complex reasoning well
- Excellent image generation with [DALL-E](https://openai.com/dall-e)

**Weaknesses:**
- Can be confidently wrong ("hallucinations")
- Privacy concerns for sensitive business data
- Expensive at scale for API usage

**Pricing:** Free tier available. Plus at $20/month. API pricing varies by model.

## [Claude](https://claude.ai) (Anthropic)

**Best for:** Analysis, long documents, coding, tasks requiring accuracy and nuance.

**Strengths:**
- Handles very long documents (up to 200K tokens)
- More careful and accurate than competitors on analytical tasks
- Excellent at following complex instructions
- Strong on Australian English and local context

**Weaknesses:**
- Smaller plugin ecosystem than ChatGPT
- No native image generation
- Can be overly cautious on some topics

**Pricing:** Free tier available. Pro at $20/month. API pricing competitive.

## [Microsoft Copilot](https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/microsoft-copilot)

**Best for:** Businesses already in the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

**Strengths:**
- Deep integration with Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams
- Works with your existing Microsoft data
- Enterprise security and compliance built in
- Natural fit for Office-heavy workflows

**Weaknesses:**
- Requires [Microsoft 365](https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/microsoft-365) subscription
- Less flexible for custom applications
- Performance varies significantly across different Office apps

**Pricing:** $30/user/month on top of Microsoft 365.

## Our recommendation

For most Australian SMBs, start with **Claude or ChatGPT** â€” both have free tiers that let you test before committing. If your business runs on Microsoft 365 and your main need is enhancing Office workflows, **Copilot** is the pragmatic choice.

For analytical work, document processing, and accuracy-critical tasks, we lean toward **Claude**. For creative content and marketing, **ChatGPT** edges ahead.

The honest answer: most businesses benefit from using more than one. They have different strengths.

## Don't overthink the choice

Once you've picked a tool, follow our step-by-step guide to [building your first AI workflow](/blog/building-your-first-ai-workflow-step-by-step-guide). For implementation pricing, see our [packages](/pricing).

The biggest mistake businesses make isn't choosing the wrong tool â€” it's spending months evaluating instead of starting. Pick one. Use it for two weeks on real work. You'll know quickly whether it fits.

We assess your specific workflows and recommend the right AI tools for your situation. The initial consultation is free. [Get in touch](mailto:ai@agenticconsciousness.com.au).
author: Daniel Hall
